schedule.description: Schedule messages to be sent at a specified time.
schedule.permissionCheck: I require the `Manage Messages` permission for the schedule command to work properly.
schedule.dynamicDesc:
Use `{ }` brackets in your message to display certain values.

**Built-in Variables:**
`{user}` — Creator mention
`{username}` — Creator's name
`{server}` — Server name
`{members}` — Member count
`{channel}` — Target channel
`{time}` — Current time
`{count}` — Recurring send count

**Custom Variables:**
`{name = "value"}` — Set a variable
Then use `{name}` anywhere after.

**Math:**
`{math=(5 + 3) * 2}` — Evaluates to `16`

**Conditions:**
`{if members > 100 do return "Big!" else return "Small"}`

**Escape:** `\{` for a literal `{`
schedule.webRecurDesc:
**Webhooks**
Send messages with a custom name & avatar.
You'll be asked during setup:
• `yes` — Set a webhook name
• `skip` — No avatar image
• The bot creates a webhook, sends, then deletes it

**Recurring Schedules**
Messages that repeat automatically:
• `none` — Send once
• `daily` — Every 24 hours
• `weekly` — Every 7 days
• `monthly` — Every 30 days
• `cron:min hour dom mon dow` — Custom

dom = day of month (1-31) | mon = month (1-12) | dow = day of week (0-6)

Example: `cron:0 9 * * 1` (Mondays at 9 AM)
*Minimum interval: 10 minutes*
schedule.embedTipsDesc:
**During embed creation, you can use these to edit your embed if you need to change anything**
`{title:...}` — Set title
`{desc:...}` — Set description
`{footer:...}` — Set footer text
`{footer_icon:URL}` — Footer icon
`{thumb:URL}` — Thumbnail image
`{image:URL}` — Main image
`{author:...}` — Author name
`{author_icon:URL}` — Author icon
`{author_url:URL}` — Author link
`{url:URL}` — Embed title link
`{color:#HEX}` — Embed color

**Tip:** Set `{prefix}timezone` for better time parsing.
**Tip:** Use `skip` to skip a stage during setup.
**Max:** Only allows to setup 10 scheduled messages at a time
schedule.stopError: You don't have an active schedule setup.
schedule.noSchedMsgs: No scheduled messages. Use `{prefix}schedule content #channel` to create one.
schedule.alreadyError: You already have an active setup. Use `{prefix}schedule stop` to cancel it.
schedule.editWhat: What would you like to edit?
schedule.editSchedLast:
React with the corresponding number to select an option.
React with ❌ to cancel.
schedule.maxSched: This server already has {maxSched} scheduled messages. Delete one before creating another.
schedule.permCheck: I don't have permission to send messages in that channel.
schedule.permCheck2: I don't have permission to view that channel.
schedule.contentMsg:
What would you like your message to be?

Send a message in chat. You can edit your message instead of restarting in case you messed something up.
schedule.embedStage:
**Current stage:** title
Send a message to set the embed's `title`
Say `skip` to skip this field.

**editing:** Use `{title:new text}`, `{desc:new text}`, etc.

**Click ✅ to schedule this embed.**
Click ❌ to cancel.
schedule.editMsgContent:
**Editing message content**

Send a new message to replace the current content.

Type `cancel` to go back to the edit menu.
schedule.editMsgEmbed:
**Editing embed fields**

Send a message using `{title:new text}`, `{desc:new text}`, `{footer:new text}`, etc.

Type `done` when finished to save and return to the menu.

remind.description: Setup a reminder to be sent at a specified time.

remind.noInput: Please provide a time and message for the reminder.
remind.noMessage: Please provide a message for the reminder.
remind.pastTime: Reminder time must be in the future.
remind.tooShort: Reminder time must be at least 1 minute in the future.

You can only have a maximum of {max} reminders at a time.

Delete some with `{cmd1}` or view them with `{cmd2}`.

Use `{cmd}` to see valid indices.
remind.deleteUsage: Please provide a valid reminder index to delete.

remind.setReminder: Set a reminder in this channel
remind.dmExplain: This deletes your message and sends you a confirmation DM
remind.dmReminder: Your reminder has been set. I'll DM you when it's time.
remind.tooFar: The time you provided is too far in the future. The maximum time is 2 years.

tempchannels.customCat: Please provide a valid category name or ID.
tempchannels.description: Setup temporary channels for your server.
tempchannels.setup:
**Setup**
Creates a default category and “Join To Create” voice channel.
tempchannels.resetSetup:
**Reset Setup**
Deletes the temporary channels setup (category, “Join To Create”, all temporary channels, and config options). Use `{default}` to delete and create new default temporary channels. Use `{config}` to outright delete everything.
tempchannels.configuration:
**Configuration**
Configure channel name, user limit, counting, and optional category
tempchannels.editConfig:
**Edit Configuration**
Modify existing temporary channel settings without resetting them
tempchannels.deleteConfig:
**Delete Configuration**
Remove specific settings from your temporary channel configuration
tempchannels.delete: Please specify which settings to delete
tempchannels.viewConfig:
**View Configuration**
Shows the current temporary channels settings.
tempchannels.nameDefine: Base name for new temporary channels.
tempchannels.limitDefine: Max users per temporary voice channel (0 = no limit).
tempchannels.countingDefine: Enables numbering on the channel name [e.g., My Channel (1)]
tempchannels.resetDefine: Required if temporary channels are already set up; deletes everything.
tempchannels.categoryDefine: Sets a custom category for the Join To Create channel. Format: `{category:<category name or id>}`
tempchannels.provideArg: Please provide a valid option for setting up temporary channels
tempchannels.validReset: Please provide a valid reset option
tempchannels.alreadySetup: This server already has temporary channels set up. If you want to continue, you need to reset the existing setup first by adding `{option}` to your command.
tempchannels.failedSetup: Failed to set up temporary channels. This usually means the bot is missing permissions such as [Manage Channels] & [Move Members]
tempchannels.successSetup: Temporary channels have been set up successfully in {voiceChannel}. You can do anything to the category and main voice channel to your liking.
tempchannels.channelNameRaw: Please provide a channel name that will be used when creating temporary channels.
tempchannels.channelNameDesc: This example will only change the name of temporary channel names to whatever text you put.
tempchannels.channelNameLength: Channel names cannot be longer than 26 characters.
tempchannels.channelLimit: Please provide a channel user limit that is equal to or below 99.
tempchannels.channelLimitDesc: This example will only change the user limit for voice channels to only allow 5 users to join.
tempchannels.notSetup: Temporary channels are not set up for this server. Use `f!tc help` to get started.
tempchannels.reset: Please provide a valid reset option
tempchannels.noReset: There is nothing to reset. Temporary channels are already disabled for this server.
tempchannels.successReset: Temporary channels have been reset. Parent/child channels and all temporary channels were removed.
tempchannels.noArgs: You must provide at least one option when using this command
tempchannels.noArgsDesc: This example will make new temporary channels only allow 5 users, be named "My Channel (Number)", custom category, and have channel management enabled.
tempchannels.manageDefine: Allows the owner of the voice channel to edit it.
tempchannels.manageFooter: When you select an emoji the bot will DM you asking for your input on editing your settings.

timezone.description: Lets you setup timezone converting in your server where if someone has their timezone set, it reacts to their message, and if the author reacts to that, it converts their time into a timestamp.
timezone.explanation:
**Explanation**
This command toggles timezone conversion on or off. When enabled, the bot scans messages for times. Once it detects one, it adds a reaction. If the message author reacts to that reaction, the bot deletes the original message and re-sends it via a webhook, using the author's name and avatar, but with any detected times converted into timestamps.
timezone.toggle:
**Toggle**
This sub-command is locked to users with [Manage Guild] permission
timezone.userTimezone:
**User Timezone Setting**
This sub-command lets any user sets their own timezone so that timezones convert correctly. Use this
timezone.userTimezoneRemove:
**User Timezone Removing**
This sub-command removes your user timezone so the bot won't react with an emoji if you post a time
timezone.validTime: Provide a valid timezone such as {timezone}, you can pick a valid timezone from this
timezone.validTime2:
Head to the website, click on the area your country resides, and it will show you your timezone. Paste that timezone in the command.
Example
timezone.sameTime: Provided timezone is the same timezone set for your current user. If you'd like to change it, head to this
timezone.successSet: Successfully setup your personal timezone to be {timezone}
timezone.noTime:
You don't have a timezone set.
You can set a timezone by going to this {timezonepicker}, copying a timezone, and using this example
timezone.viewCmd:
**View**
View your or another user's timezone info
timezone.optionalUser: User's name, id, mention or timezone, e.g. America/New_York
timezone.memberNotFound: Provide a user's username, mention, id, or a timezone to view information.
timezone.noTimeSelf:
You don't have a timezone set.
You can set a timezone by going to this {timezonepicker}, copying a timezone, and using this example
timezone.noUsers: No users in this server have this timezone set.

ping.description: Just shows ping for the bot. That's all

bypass.description: Grant roles a bypass to use commands locked behind permissions.
bypass.roleName: You must provide a role's name. Example
bypass.provideCommands: You must provide commands to bypass for **{role}**. Example
bypass.explanation:
**Explanation**
This is a help menu for bypassing roles. Users with bypassed roles can be given access to commands normally permission locked. You can pick specific commands or **all** which bypasses all commands.
bypass.maximumBypass: You've reached the maximum amount of bypassed roles per server (15 roles)
bypass.roleAlready:
You provided a role that already has a bypass
**Bypass Commands**
bypass.roleUse: Use this to edit the role
bypass.invalidCmds: You provided invalid commands to be added to the bypass. Remove them and try again.
bypass.successAll: Successfully gave bypass to ALL permission locked commands to
bypass.success: Successfully gave {role} bypass to these commands
bypass.notBypassed: Provided role isn't bypassed so I can't remove it.
bypass.notBypassedEdit: Provided role isn't bypassed so I can't edit it.
bypass.noRolesView: There's no bypassed roles to view.

autoroles.description: Setup autoroles in your server such as join roles or sticky roles.
autoroles.explanation:
**Explanation**
This is a help menu to show all available sub-commands this command has. Use these to setup autoroles in your server.
autoroles.view:
**View**
This sub-command lets you view all your autorole settings.
autoroles.stickyRoles:
**Sticky Roles**
This sub-command toggles sticky roles which save user's roles when they leave or kicked from the server, and add them back on join.
autoroles.joinRoles:
**Join Roles**
This sub-command setups join roles where it adds roles to users when they join.
autoroles.noOptionsAdd:
Provide role names to be added to autoroles.
Example
autoroles.noOptionsRemove:
Provide role names to be removed from autoroles.
Example
autoroles.sticky: Successfully changed sticky roles to be turned
autoroles.tooMany: You provided too many roles. Join roles can only have a maximum of 20 roles in total.
autoroles.completeAdd:
Successfully added autoroles when users join.
Roles:
autoroles.completeRemove:
Successfully removed autoroles when users join.
Roles:
autoroles.roleErrorsAdd:
All of the roles you provided are duplicates, please provide new roles.
Roles:
autoroles.roleErrorsRemove:
None of the roles you provided are join roles, please provide join roles.
Roles:
autoroles.someDuplicates: These roles are already join roles:
autoroles.noTimeOption: No time was provided. Please provide a time, e.g. `{time:10m}`
autoroles.timedRoles:
**Timed Roles**
This sub-command setups timed roles where it adds roles to users after a specific amount of time.

roles.description: Setup a reaction role message for your server
roles.reactionFixError: Failed to remove reactions from the old message. Please check that the bot has the necessary permissions.
roles.already: You already have an active message collector going on! Finish that first before starting a new one. If you would like to turn that collector off, run `{prefix}roles stop`
roles.pick: Provide what you would like the type of message to be sent. Options: `embed`, `content`
roles.ended: The message collector has ended after 25 minutes.
roles.success: Completed reaction role message was sent to
roles.react:
Send a message you would like to be as your reaction role message. Make sure to put roles following this format: `{role:Blue}` in your message wherever you would like them to be at. To cancel this reaction role collector, react with ❌ to stop it!

**Example**:
{example}
Once the bot replaces your message, react to that new message with emojis in order of the first roles in your message. So if you reacted with :blue_circle:, it will replace `{role:blue}` with ":blue_circle: Blue"

After you reacted with all of your emojis, react with ✅ to complete the reaction collector.
roles.noPerms: I require the `Manage Roles` permission to add roles to users.
roles.max: You can only have a maximum of 12 reaction role messages in your server.
roles.noRoles: There are no reaction role messages setup currently. To add some, type
roles.delete: Provide an ID for the reaction role message to delete it
roles.edit: Provide an ID for the reaction role message to edit it
roles.notFound: I could not find a reaction role message with that ID.
roles.deleted: Successfully deleted that reaction role message.
roles.explain2: When you create a reaction message (`{prefix}roles create`), you will need to provide it with roles (format: `{role:Blue}`) and I will copy your message. React to my message to automatically update the roles and once you are finished, react with ✅ to complete it.
roles.reactionFixExplain: This sub-command fixes the reactions on a reaction role message.
roles.fix: Provide an ID for a reaction role message to fix the reactions on it
roles.noPerms2: I require the `Manage Messages` permission to remove reactions to that message.
roles.stopped: Successfully stopped your current reaction role collector!
roles.notStarted: You have not started a reaction role collector yet!
roles.exclusive: Provide a message ID to toggle exclusive mode.
roles.exclusiveExplain: Exclusive mode limits the reaction message to only give the user one role.
roles.mentionExplain: If you add `{mention}` anywhere in your setup message after running `{prefix}roles create`, it will make the bot mention the roles instead of only showing the role's names on the message. You can put it anywhere as `{mention}` will be removed.
roles.reactMention: **Role Mentions:** include `{mention}` anywhere in your message and the bot will mention roles instead of only putting the role's name.

second.end: To get more information about a command, type

language.description: Changes the language of the bot
language.change: You can change your server's language by picking an available language.

schedule.fieldsDone:
**All fields completed**

Use `{field:new alue}` (e.g. `{title:new text}`) to edit any field.
schedule.editMode:
**Current Stage:** {stage}
Send a message to set the embed's `{stage}`.
Say `skip` to move to the next field.

You can also use `{title:new text}`, `{desc:new text}`, `{footer:new text}`, etc.
schedule.menu: Type `done` to save and return to the menu.
schedule.newMsg:
**Click ✅ to schedule this embed.**
Click ❌ to cancel.
schedule.askWebhook:
**Would you like to send this as a webhook?**

Webhooks let you send messages with a custom name and avatar.

Type `yes` to set up a webhook, or `no` to skip.

You can also type `cancel` to cancel the schedule setup.
schedule.webhookName:
**What name should the webhook use?**

Send a message with the name you want. Maximum of 80 characters.
Type `skip` to use the bot's name
Type `cancel` to cancel the setup.
schedule.webhookURL:
**What avatar URL should the webhook use?**

Send a link to an image (must start with `https://` and end with png, jpg, etc.)

Type `skip` to use the default bot avatar.
Type `cancel` to cancel the setup.
schedule.repeatOften:
**How often should this repeat?**

Type one of the following options
• `none` - Send once (Default)
• `daily` - Repeat every day
• `weekly` - Repeat every week
• `monthly` - Repeat every month
• `cron:min hour dom mon dow` - Custom cron schedule

dom = day of month (1-31) | mon = month (1-12) | dow = day of week (0-6)
Example: `cron:0 9 * * 1` (every Monday at 9 AM)

Type `cancel` to cancel the setup.
schedule.invalidURL: Invalid URL. Please provide a valid image URL starting with `https://` or type `skip`.
schedule.invalidCron:
Invalid cron expression. Use format: `cron:min hour dom mon dow`

dom = day of month (1-31) | mon = month (1-12) | dow = day of week (0-6)
schedule.miniCron: Minimum cron interval is every 10 minutes(`cron:*/10 * * * *`).
schedule.invalidOption: Invalid option. Choose `none`, `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`, or a `cron:` expression.
schedule.invalidTime: Invalid time format. Please use something like
schedule.futureTime: Provided time is in the past. Please use a future time to proceed.
schedule.success: Message scheduled. It will be sent {time} in {channel}.

manageVC.stopped: Cancelled the message collector. React to the voice channel menu to start a new one.
manageVC.invalidLimit: Invalid limit text. Make sure to provide a number when setting the limit.
manageVC.invalidUser: Invalid user. Make sure to provide a user's mention or ID.
manageVC.blockUserDesc:
{username} has been blocked from joining your voice channel.

**User:** {userMention}
manageVC.userAccessDesc:
{username} can now join your voice channel.

**User:** {userMention}
manageVC.renameChannelDesc: Type the new name for your voice channel and send it.
manageVC.setLimitDesc:
Type a number to set how many users can join your voice channel.

**0** = Unlimited users
**1-99** = That many users max
manageVC.setBlockDesc:
Type a user's mention (@username) or ID to prevent them from joining your voice channel.

If they are currently in your channel, they will be removed.
manageVC.allowUserDesc:
Type a user's mention (@username) or ID to let them join your voice channel.

This will remove any block on the user.
manageVC.isPrivate: Your voice channel is now private. Only you can join.
manageVC.isNotPrivate: Your voice channel is now public. Everyone can join.
manageVC.dmsOff: if you want to edit your Voice Channel settings then enable Direct Messages for this server.
manageVC.changeRegionDesc:
Type the new region for your voice channel and send it.
**Available Regions**:
manageVC.transferOwnerDesc: Type the user's mention (@username) or ID to transfer ownership to them.
manageVC.closeChannelDesc: Are you sure you want to close your channel? To confirm type `confirm`
manageVC.invalidRegion: Provided region is invalid. Make sure to type an available region listed above.
manageVC.noBots: You cannot give voice channel ownership to a bot. Make sure to provide a user's mention or ID.
manageVC.closeConfirm: Invalid response. To confirm closing your VC, type `confirm`
